remaining_gaps.description: Governance conformance remains partial because required pre-commit hook tokens are missing in the system hook surface.
Root pre-commit hook evidence is missing expected governance tokens.

AiGOL can govern and certify implementation requests, worker requests, execution candidates, execution results, validation, and replay certification, but code changes are still implemented by Codex-assisted development.
Human approval remains mandatory at governance gates; this is constitutionally desired but remains an operational dependency for throughput.

remaining_risks.description: Partial governance conformance must remain visible during adoption; it cannot be reframed as full constitutional conformance.
ACLI lifecycle certification depends on correct operator prompt sequencing and copied next actions.
AiGOL currently produces governed implementation and worker artifacts, but Codex still performs repository edits and test execution.
Production readiness is for supervised Sapianta development governance, not autonomous constitutional mutation or unrestricted execution.

remaining_manual_dependencies: Human approval at PPP candidate, implementation request, dispatch candidate, invocation candidate, execution candidate, and governed execution gates.
Human review of readiness reports, certification decisions, and release promotion.
Operator sequencing for full ACLI lifecycle prompts where copied next actions are still required.
Manual remediation of governance hook conformance drift.
Manual decision to promote local PC artifacts into governed GitHub release registry.

dependencies: Repository code edits and test creation.
Runtime scaffolding for new governance capabilities.
Report generation when no dedicated AiGOL report writer exists.
Manual interpretation of conformance drift and adoption recommendations.
non_dependencies: Replay lineage preservation inside certified runtimes.
Fail-closed validation behavior inside certified runtimes.
ACLI lifecycle execution and replay artifact generation once runtime code is present.

migration_recommendations.name: Supervised Primary Governance
migration_recommendations.recommendation: Use AiGOL as the primary governance record and certification layer for Sapianta development while Codex remains the implementation executor.
entry_criteria: ACLI regression certification remains at termination_rate >= 0.95.

migration_recommendations.name: Conformance Closure
migration_recommendations.recommendation: Resolve hook conformance drift before claiming full production governance readiness.
entry_criteria: No HIGH severity governance conformance warnings remain.

migration_recommendations.name: Governed Implementation Queue
migration_recommendations.recommendation: Route Sapianta work through AiGOL-generated implementation requests and worker request artifacts before Codex implementation.
entry_criteria: PPP candidate, implementation request, and worker request artifacts are required for non-trivial changes.
Replay certification is attached to closed-loop improvement proposals.

production_adoption_plan.action: Adopt AiGOL as the required governance evidence layer for Sapianta development requests.
Require replay certification and ACLI regression certification for promotion-relevant runtime changes.
Keep Codex as the implementation agent, with AiGOL artifacts serving as the governing intake, approval, execution, validation, and certification spine.
Remediate governance hook conformance drift before broadening claims from conditional readiness to full production readiness.

blockers: HIGH severity governance hook conformance drift
HIGH Codex implementation dependency for repository mutation
